input.tenant_message: The dryer has been making a really loud squealing and thumping sound when it runs. It still dries clothes okay but the noise is terrible — it sounds like metal grinding on metal every time the drum goes around. It started about a month ago and has been getting progressively worse. I'm worried it might stop working soon or even become a fire hazard.

ai_output.recommended_action: Schedule a technician to inspect the dryer drum support rollers, drum glides, belt, and idler pulley — these are the most common causes of squealing and thumping sounds. Metal-on-metal grinding can accelerate wear and eventually cause belt or drum failure. Repair before the noise worsens to prevent a more costly replacement.
